summary:
The anteroposterior (AP) X-ray provides limited ability to diagnose spondylolisthesis since it fails to accurately measure vertebral slippage; lateral imaging is superior for both identification and quantitative evaluation based on standardized metrics such as the Meyerding classification.  
$\boxed{\text{C}}$

$\boxed{\text{C}}$. Answer choice: C
